sections.title: What It Is
bullets: Large-scale technology expo: AI & Big Data Expo Global sits alongside other TechEx tracks (e.g. IoT, cyber, DevOps), so you get a wide vendor and practitioner ecosystem in one venue.
Heavy on expo floor, keynotes, and track sessions aimed at organisations evaluating or scaling data and AI platforms.
Strong fit if you want to compare enterprise stacks, meet systems integrators, and read near-term buyer priorities.

sections.title: Who You're Likely to Meet
bullets: CIOs, CTOs, heads of data and analytics, and programme owners for AI and modernisation.
Cloud and data platform vendors, analytics consultancies, and implementation partners hunting enterprise deals.
Technical architects and data engineers tasked with turning strategy into pipelines, governance, and production ML.

sections.title: What It Is
bullets: Gartner-run forum aimed at senior IT leadership: strategy, operating model, risk, and technology investment choices—including AI as a core theme alongside broader digital priorities.
Agenda blends Gartner analyst frameworks with CIO peer discussion; less of a trade-show floor, more executive problem-solving.
Useful for calibrating how large enterprises govern AI spend, vendor selection, and organisational change.

sections.title: Who You're Likely to Meet
bullets: CIOs and senior IT leaders from mid-market and large enterprises across sectors.
Gartner advisors and invited speakers shaping how boards expect IT to report on AI value and risk.
Vendor executives sometimes present, but the centre of gravity is buyer-side leadership.

sections.title: What It Is
bullets: Engineering-first conference focused on building with LLMs: tooling, evaluation, agents, and production patterns rather than high-level strategy decks alone.
Typically draws people shipping code and systems—workshops and deep talks outweigh generic “future of AI” keynotes.
Strong focus on what is technically feasible now versus slide-ware-only messaging.

sections.title: Who You're Likely to Meet
bullets: Staff engineers, ML/platform leads, and technical founders building AI products.
Developer-tool vendors and API providers who want hands-on users and design partners.
Some investors scouting teams with execution depth; hiring managers looking for senior ICs.

sections.title: What It Is
bullets: Enterprise-oriented summit (IQPC / AI Data Analytics Network family) centred on generative AI adoption, use cases, and operating models.
Strong emphasis on business cases, governance, and organisational change as GenAI moves from pilots to wider rollout.
Audience skews toward leaders who must justify GenAI budgets to boards and regulators.

sections.title: Who You're Likely to Meet
bullets: Digital, innovation, and transformation heads plus line-of-business sponsors of AI initiatives.
Risk, compliance, and data governance stakeholders increasingly pulled into GenAI programmes.
Consultancies and vendors targeting enterprise rollouts rather than hobbyist builders.

sections.title: What It Is
bullets: Conference focused on knowledge management, enterprise search, content, and intelligent information access—areas that overlap heavily with RAG, copilots, and internal AI assistants.
Often attracts owners of intranets, document repositories, and customer-support knowledge bases modernising with AI.
Pre-conference workshops sometimes dive deeper into tooling and methods.

sections.title: Who You're Likely to Meet
bullets: Heads of knowledge, information management, and enterprise search; increasingly data leaders involved in unstructured data strategy.
Vendors in search, content services, and document AI; integrators connecting legacy ECM to new AI layers.
Practitioners responsible for findability, compliance, and knowledge retention at scale.

sections.title: What It Is
bullets: Free, high-traffic AWS regional summit: keynotes, hundreds of breakout sessions, and a large expo focused on building and running workloads on AWS—including AI/ML services.
Single-day format with a broad cross-section of industries and skill levels.
Strong on product roadmaps, architecture patterns, and partner ecosystem visibility.

sections.title: Who You're Likely to Meet
bullets: Cloud engineers, platform teams, and solutions architects from enterprises standardising on AWS.
AWS partners (consultancies, ISVs) and recruiters hunting cloud/ML talent.
IT leaders scanning how their teams should adopt managed AI services versus bespoke stacks.

sections.title: What It Is
bullets: Gartner’s flagship data and analytics conference: strategy for D&A leadership, AI embedded across analytics, governance, and value measurement.
Heavy focus on how CDAOs align with CIOs/CFOs and prove impact.
Provides frameworks for data product thinking, AI governance, and vendor landscape navigation.

sections.title: Who You're Likely to Meet
bullets: CDAOs, heads of data science/engineering, and senior analytics leaders.
Gartner analysts and invited executives discussing enterprise-wide data monetisation and AI risk.
Technology providers targeting the office of the CDAO.

sections.title: What It Is
bullets: Single-day conference focused on practical AI in business, often co-located with digital transformation programming so attendees span tech and business roles.
Sessions tend toward adoption stories, operating models, and change management rather than pure research.
Compact format suits focused networking without multi-day fatigue.

sections.title: Who You're Likely to Meet
bullets: Business leaders sponsoring AI initiatives alongside IT counterparts.
Transformation and programme directors juggling vendors and internal stakeholders.
Mid-sized enterprise delegates who want actionable takeaways more than vendor theatre.

sections.title: What It Is
bullets: Tech Summit–branded AI conference emphasising enterprise innovation, leadership, and technology strategy.
Typically mixes keynotes and panels aimed at senior decision-makers responsible for AI and digital agendas.
Single-day structure keeps the audience concentrated.

sections.title: Who You're Likely to Meet
bullets: CIOs, CTOs, and heads of AI from organisations modernising core systems and customer channels.
Technology vendors and regional consultancies targeting leadership attendees.
Innovation and partnership leads scouting emerging suppliers.

sections.title: What It Is
bullets: Gartner event for CFOs and finance leadership with a growing emphasis on AI-driven forecasting, close automation, risk analytics, and digital finance operating models.
Combines strategic keynotes with Xpo floor exposure to finance technology providers.
Strong lens on governance, controls, and ROI—how finance should oversee enterprise AI spend.

sections.title: Who You're Likely to Meet
bullets: CFOs, finance transformation leaders, controllers, and FP&A executives.
Procurement and IT finance partners involved in enterprise software decisions.
Vendors selling ERP adjacency, close tools, and AI analytics for finance.

sections.title: What It Is
bullets: London Tech Week (LTW) is the UK’s flagship annual technology festival—run as a major Informa Tech / Informa Festivals programme—built around a central expo and content stages at Olympia London plus a city-wide fringe of partner and satellite events.

The 2026 edition is publicised as 8–12 June: core expo and main stage programming 8–10 June at Olympia, with a broader fringe programme advertised across London through 12 June, so the week mixes a concentrated “main campus” experience with dozens of distributed meetups, breakfasts, and sector sessions.

Positioning is deliberately cross-ecosystem: enterprise adoption, startups and scale-ups, capital (VC, CVC, growth equity), and public-policy / institutional voices—so AI appears throughout, alongside cloud, cyber, fintech, climate-tech, and general digital transformation narratives rather than a single narrow vertical.

sections.title: Who You're Likely to Meet
bullets: C-suite and functional leaders (CIO, CTO, CDO, CISO, CPO, CFO office) plus heads of innovation, transformation, and AI centres of excellence from UK and international organisations.
Founders, startup operators, and accelerator cohorts; investors and corporate venture teams scouting deals and partnerships; Big Tech and hyperscaler regional leadership often visible on main stages.

Government, regulators, and trade-body figures in UK tech and digital policy slots—useful for reading how AI governance, safety, and competitiveness narratives are being framed for enterprise audiences.

Press, analysts, and recruiters—expect high footfall and noisy floors; serious buyers usually pre-book meetings and anchor around specific sub-events (e.g. The AI Summit London, investor lounges, invitation-only roundtables).

sections.title: Format, Scale & How to Plan
bullets: Scale is large by design (the programme markets tens of thousands of attendees, hundreds of speakers, and 70+ discrete events)—treat LTW as a portfolio week: pick a small set of must-see stages and fringe sessions rather than trying to “cover everything.”

Venue split matters: Olympia anchors the trade-show and headline content; Tobacco Dock and other London venues host major co-located conferences during the same week—check calendars for overlaps and travel time between sites.

Enterprise value is highest when you align attendance to a clear objective: vendor shortlists, partnership sourcing, talent market sensing, or policy risk scanning—then use the official agenda, app, and side-event lists to protect calendar bandwidth.

sections.title: What It Is
bullets: Major commercial AI conference: large expo, multi-track agenda, and significant vendor and enterprise buyer traffic.
Coverage spans applied AI across industries—vision, language, automation, and platform plays.
High energy and volume; think curated serendipity if you plan well.

sections.title: Who You're Likely to Meet
bullets: Enterprise IT and AI leaders, innovation teams, and startup founders seeking enterprise customers.
Global technology vendors, cloud providers, and systems integrators with large booths.
Press, analysts, and investors scanning market momentum.

sections.title: What It Is
bullets: Google Cloud summit showcasing infrastructure, data, analytics, and AI services—Gemini, Vertex AI, and customer stories on running workloads on GCP.
Mix of technical breakouts and executive content aimed at existing and prospective GCP customers.
Strong partner ecosystem presence.

sections.title: Who You're Likely to Meet
bullets: Cloud architects, data engineers, and AI leads on GCP or evaluating multi-cloud strategies.
Google Cloud partners and specialised consultancies.
Business owners seeking regulated, data-rich AI patterns on Google’s stack.

sections.title: What It Is
bullets: Broad AI conference positioning at the intersection of research, enterprise deployment, and emerging tech narratives.
Programme typically mixes strategic keynotes with more technical streams on implementation and integration.
Two-day format allows deeper exposure than single-day pop-ups.

sections.title: Who You're Likely to Meet
bullets: CTOs, heads of innovation, and senior technologists evaluating AI roadmaps.
Academics and advanced practitioners alongside vendor strategists.
International delegates; useful for cross-border benchmarking and partner scouting.

sections.title: What It Is
bullets: Reuters Events–style executive conference emphasising enterprise AI strategy, value realisation, and governance narratives trusted by large organisations.
Agenda often connects AI to risk, compliance, and operational resilience—themes familiar to regulated industries.
High production quality and senior speaker line-ups.

sections.title: Who You're Likely to Meet
bullets: CIOs, CDOs, CTOs, and strategy officers from enterprises under public and regulatory scrutiny.
Consultancies and advisory firms supporting board-level AI programmes.
Technology vendors targeting top-down enterprise sales motions.

sections.title: What It Is
bullets: RE•WORK summit focused on AI applications in banking, markets, insurance, and fintech—fraud, credit, customer experience, and automation.
Single-track or focused multi-track days with deep practitioner talks.
Smaller than mega-expos; higher density of domain specialists.

sections.title: Who You're Likely to Meet
bullets: ML leads, data scientists, and innovation heads inside financial institutions.
Regtech and core technology vendors selling into finance.
Startups seeking proofs of concept with banks and insurers.

sections.title: What It Is
bullets: BizClik-backed summit and awards programme focusing on generative AI, automation, and enterprise transformation storytelling.
Blends conference content with recognition of teams and vendors—useful for scanning who markets themselves as leaders.
Strong media and brand presence around the event footprint.

sections.title: Who You're Likely to Meet
bullets: C-suite and senior leaders interested in visibility and thought leadership as well as procurement.
Marketing and communications leaders alongside technology owners.
Sponsors and partners using the event for brand and lead generation.

sections.title: What It Is
bullets: Later edition of the AI & Big Data Expo Global series—same broad TechEx ecosystem of enterprise technology tracks and expo format.
Programme details are updated as the organiser publishes each wave of sessions and co-located tracks.
Expect a similar mix of vendors, practitioners, and multi-track sessions to recent years.

sections.title: Who You're Likely to Meet
bullets: Likely similar to other editions in this series: senior technology and data leaders, architects, and a wide partner ecosystem.
Vendors preparing multi-year enterprise pipeline development.
Teams aligning multi-year partner and pipeline plans with major expo cycles.

sections.title: What It Is
bullets: RE•WORK summit aimed at Chief AI Officers and senior leaders responsible for enterprise AI strategy, scaling, and governance.
Programming emphasises cross-functional alignment, risk management, and building durable AI capabilities—not one-off pilots.
Often includes optional workshops or add-on days beyond the core summit—worth scanning the full programme structure.

sections.title: Who You're Likely to Meet
bullets: CAIOs or equivalent heads of AI centres of excellence, plus legal, risk, and data counterparts they bring along.
Senior vendor strategists targeting top-down AI programmes.
Peers comparing operating models for centralised vs federated AI teams.

sections.title: What It Is
bullets: Following edition of the CIO Leadership Forum from Gartner—same general format with refreshed research and priority themes.
Expect analyst-led sessions on IT leadership, digital business, AI implications for enterprise architecture, and risk.
Official agenda shows how emphasis shifts year on year (AI security, vendor management, talent models, etc.).

sections.title: Who You're Likely to Meet
bullets: CIOs and senior IT executives planning multi-year technology roadmaps.
Gartner speakers and facilitators shaping enterprise buying narratives.
Select technology and services providers in sponsor capacities.

sections.title: What It Is
bullets: Corinium’s CDAO UK gathering for chief data and analytics officers and senior data/AI executives—peer-driven agendas, roundtables, and case-led sessions.
Focus on aligning data strategy, analytics value, and AI enablement with enterprise goals.
Format stays heavy on roundtables and closed-door peer sessions rather than a giant expo floor.

sections.title: Who You're Likely to Meet
bullets: CDAOs, CDOs, heads of analytics/engineering, and their direct reports from large UK and international firms.
Technology and services firms targeting the office of the chief data officer.
Peers exchanging playbooks on data mesh, governance, and AI productisation.
